title: myrrhis brevistylis
intro: Myrrhis brevistylis, also known as Alpine caraway or Short-styled Myrrh, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It is a member of the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, colds, and respiratory infections, and its health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Myrrhis brevistylis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, and its essential oil is also used in aromatherapy and perfumery.
